rule_metadata.author: RedCanary, Sigma formatting by Micah Babinski
rule_metadata.title: PowerShell Base64 Encoding

rules.rule_text:
title: PowerShell Base64 Encoding
id: df33cc09-ea03-4681-bd6c-b8dba7328a84
status: experimental
description: "This analytic looks for the execution of a process that seems to be\
  \ \npowershell.exe along with a corresponding command line containing the \nterm\
  \ base64. Base64 encoding isn\u2019t inherently suspicious, but it\u2019s worth\
  \ \nlooking out for in a lot of environments, and the following pseudo-detection\
  \ \nlogic can help detect a wide variety of malicious activity. Part of the \nRedCanary\
  \ 2024 Threat Detection Report.\n"
references:
- https://redcanary.com/threat-detection-report/techniques/powershell/
author: RedCanary, Sigma formatting by Micah Babinski
date: 2024/03/21
tags:
- attack.execution
- attack.t1059
- attack.t1059.001
- attack.defense_evasion
- attack.t1027
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith: \powershell.exe
    CommandLine|contains: base64
  condition: selection
falsepositives:
- Unknown
level: low
